title: GH-137218: Fix unnecessary recompile of `Programs/_freeze_module`
author: savannahostrowski
body:
<!--
Thanks for your contribution!
Please read this comment in its entirety. It's quite important.

# Pull Request title

It should be in the following format:

```
gh-NNNNNN: Summary of the changes made
```

Where: gh-NNNNNN refers to the GitHub issue number.

Most PRs will require an issue number. Trivial changes, like fixing a typo, do not need an issue.

# Backport Pull Request title

If this is a backport PR (PR made against branches other than `main`),
please ensure that the PR title is in the following format:

```
[X.Y] <title from the original PR> (GH-NNNNNN)
```

Where: [X.Y] is the branch name, for example: [3.13].

GH-NNNNNN refers to the PR number from `main`.

-->

As @encukou [pointed out](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137218#issuecomment-3318716302), running `make` twice after merging https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/139170 resulted in an unnecessary recompile. I believe this is because of a redundant assignment of `JIT_STENCILS_H`.


<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-137218 -->
* Issue: gh-137218
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

comments.author: savannahostrowski
comments.body: @encukou Did you want to take a look at this one?

comments.author: encukou
comments.body:
Sorry, I missed the notification!

Unfortunately this doesn't solve the issue for me. In my config, `REGEN_JIT_COMMAND` is empty, so a `jit_stencils.h` is never created, but `make` attempts to create it and recreates its dependents (`jit_stencils.h` → `Python/jit.o` → `Programs/_freeze_module` → frozen modules).

comments.author: savannahostrowski
comments.body: Okay, I think that removing the literal `jit_stencils.h` dependency fixes this. This should also still work just fine to solve the original problem since the platform-specific stencils always exist alongside `jit_stencils.h`.
